description: Two-track cleanup: (1) remove all dead/unused code, variables, functions, and imports across the codebase; (2) fix all 170 failing unit tests across 21 test files so the full suite is green.

userStories.title: Remove all unused code, variables, functions, files, and flows
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the codebase free of all unused code so it is easier to maintain, navigate, and reason about.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run lint passes with 0 errors
npm run build passes with 0 TypeScript errors
No unused imports or variables remain in src/ or tests/
No exported functions or components exist that are never imported anywhere
No source files exist that are never imported anywhere (dead files)
No unreachable code blocks or dead conditional branches remain
No commented-out blocks of code remain (comments explaining why are fine)
No feature flags, constants, or config values that are never read remain
userStories.notes: Multi-step approach: (1) Run `npm run lint` — fix all ESLint unused-var violations. (2) Run `npm run build` — fix all TypeScript unused variable errors. (3) For each file in src/: grep for its exports across the whole codebase to find any that are never imported — delete or consolidate. (4) For each src/ file: check if the file itself is ever imported anywhere — if not, delete it. (5) Search for dead conditional flows: feature flags, env vars, or boolean constants that are always one value. (6) Search for commented-out code blocks (multi-line // or /* */ blocks containing logic). Use `grep -r` carefully and verify before deleting — a function only used in tests still counts as used. Do NOT remove code that is exported and imported elsewhere. After all removals, re-run lint and build to confirm clean.

userStories.title: Fix shared test mock infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the shared Supabase mock in tests/utils.tsx to support all query builder methods used in production code so tests don't fail due to missing mock methods.
acceptanceCriteria: createMockQueryBuilder in tests/utils.tsx includes a chainable limit() method
The Supabase client mock includes an rpc() method that returns a mock response
npm run test:run -- tests/unit/components/home/HomeSection.test.tsx no longer throws 'rpc is not a function'
userStories.notes: tests/utils.tsx contains createMockQueryBuilder — add `limit: vi.fn().mockReturnThis()` to the returned object alongside select/insert/eq etc. The HomeSection test fails with 'supabase.rpc is not a function' — add `rpc: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue({ data: [], error: null })` to the supabase mock object. pantry.test.ts fails because getPantryItems likely uses .limit() internally. Run each test file after fixing to confirm.

userStories.title: Fix userPreferences and Settings tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the userPreferences and Settings tests to match the current schema so they pass reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run test:run -- tests/unit/lib/userPreferences.test.ts passes (all 8 tests)
npm run test:run -- tests/unit/pages/Settings.test.tsx passes (all tests)
userStories.notes: userPreferences.test.ts expects `aiModelCombine` and `aiModelParse` fields but the production code now uses a single `aiModel` field. Update test expectations to match. Settings.test.tsx likely fails because its mock of @/lib/userPreferences does not export `getCachedAiModel` — check what exports the actual module has and update the mock accordingly. Read src/lib/userPreferences.ts first to understand the current shape, then update the tests.

userStories.title: Fix MealPlanPage tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want MealPlanPage tests to reflect the current component implementation so all ~63 tests pass.
userStories.notes: MealPlanPage was refactored to use the shared useRecipeParse hook (src/hooks/useRecipeParse.ts). The parse state, effect, and handlers (handleParseRetry, handleParseKeep, handleParseDiscard) were extracted to the hook. Tests that stub or assert on those internal functions may need updating. Also, loadPlan likely uses .limit() — this should be fixed by US-002. Read MealPlanPage.test.tsx and MealPlanPage.tsx to understand what changed, then update tests. Many tests may just need the mock query builder to have limit() (US-002 prerequisite).

userStories.title: Fix EventRatingDialog tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all EventRatingDialog tests to match the current component so they pass.
userStories.notes: All EventRatingDialog tests are failing. Read the current src/components/events/EventRatingDialog.tsx to understand its current props/behavior, then read tests/unit/components/events/EventRatingDialog.test.tsx to see what's out of date. Update the tests to match the current component API and rendered output.

userStories.title: Fix GroceryExportMenu, GroceryListSection, and RecipeHub tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want recipe/grocery component tests to match the current UI so they pass.
userStories.notes: GroceryExportMenu tests look for 'CSV' text that no longer exists in the component — read GroceryExportMenu.tsx to see current button labels and update tests. GroceryListSection tests may have drifted due to the Select dropdown added for mobile in the UX polish pass. RecipeHub tests fail on the new Add Recipe dialog tests — investigate specific failures by running the test file and reading error output. All three files are in src/components/recipes/.

userStories.title: Fix auth, GoogleSignIn, and HomeSection tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want auth-related tests to reflect the current component and library implementations.
userStories.notes: auth.test.ts has 1 failure in signOut. GoogleSignIn.test.tsx has ~10 failures in the dev-mode email auth describe block. Read each source file (src/lib/auth.ts, src/components/auth/GoogleSignIn.tsx) and compare with tests to identify drift. HomeSection's rpc error should be fixed by US-002.

userStories.title: Fix MealPlanGrid, RecipeClubEvents, and RecipeIngredientList tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want these component tests to match the current implementations.
userStories.notes: MealPlanGrid has 3 failures around mealTypes prop and mobile column headers. RecipeClubEvents has 1 failure in the loading spinner test. RecipeIngredientList has 2 failures. For each: read the source component, run the test, identify the mismatch, update the test.

userStories.title: Fix page-level tests (ContactUs, Index, EventDetailPage, PersonalMealDetailPage, UserManagementPage)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all page-level tests to match the current page implementations.
userStories.notes: For each failing page: run the specific test file to see error messages, read the source page file, identify what changed, update the test. ContactUs has 7 failures. Index has 4. EventDetailPage has widespread failures (entire describe block). PersonalMealDetailPage has 9 failures — likely related to the useRecipeParse hook refactor (same as MealPlanPage). UserManagementPage has 1 failure. PersonalMealDetailPage.tsx uses the same parse dialog pattern — check if it also uses useRecipeParse now.

userStories.title: Fix remaining lib and edge-function tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all remaining lib and edge-function tests to pass.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run test:run -- tests/unit/lib/pantry.test.ts passes (after US-002)
npm run test:run passes with 0 failures
userStories.notes: pantry.test.ts (2 failures) should be fixed by US-002 limit() fix. parseIngredientText.test.ts has 1 failure in 'invokes parse-recipe without recipeId'. recipeActions.test.ts has 1 failure in saveRecipeEdit. parse-recipe edge function test has 1 failure for missing recipeId 400 response. For each: run the test, read the error, read the source, update the test or fix the source as appropriate. Final criterion is full suite green.
